primary_factors: Ice/black-ice setup at very cold pavement temps (mid-teens): even trace freezing rain can create hazardous secondary-road and neighborhood conditions
Freezing precip timing is close to bus dispatch (light freezing rain/drizzle around ~4–6 AM; most guidance has it ending by ~7 AM, but a 15–25% chance it lingers into 7–8 AM)
Michigan operational response likely favors treatment + possible DELAY rather than full closure when precip is ending near commute
No confirmed Rockford or neighboring-district closures yet; FOX17 closings list currently shows no active closings/delays (reduces domino-pressure)
Elevated but localized power-outage tail risk (statewide outages high; Rockford/Kent not clearly a hardest-hit area, but any building outage/heat loss could force a closure)
decision_rationale:
All experts converged near ~20% for a FULL closure, and the follow-up checks did not uncover new closure signals. Meteorology + Safety + History agree this is an ICE/patchy glaze risk at ~14–16°F (not a snow-accumulation event), with the most operationally sensitive period near 4–6:30 AM. That supports meaningful disruption risk, but Michigan districts often manage this by pre-treating and/or calling a 2-hour delay—especially when guidance suggests precip is ending by ~7 AM. News intel currently shows no active closings/delays on FOX17 and no Rockford post yet, so the domino effect is not engaged. Power-grid conditions add a non-trivial tail risk (large statewide outages), but without evidence Rockford is specifically impacted, it is not enough to push to a high closure probability. Net: full closure remains a minority outcome driven by (a) unexpectedly widespread glaze on secondary roads during 5–7 AM route checks and/or (b) significant localized outages affecting buildings or key intersections.

Operational recommendation: treat as a likely OPEN or 2-hour DELAY morning, with the closure probability at 22% primarily due to black-ice risk plus outage tail risk.

collaboration.summary: Agents reached consensus after 2 round(s) with 2.0% spread. Average probability: 20%.
